news.headline: Form 144: RPID insider notifies sale of 10,000 Class A shares (option exercise)
news.summary: An RPID insider filed a Form 144 to sell 10,000 Class A shares obtained via option exercise, the latest in a string of small routine insider sales also dated June 10, June 22 and July 1, 2026.

news.headline: Rapid Micro Biosystems Announces Inducement Grant Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
news.summary: The company granted 60,000 restricted stock units to a newly hired employee as a material inducement to employment, vesting in three equal annual installments over three years.

news.headline: KeyBanc Capital Markets cuts Rapid Micro Biosystems price target to $5 from $8
news.summary: KeyBanc lowered its price target on RPID to $5 from $8, citing the dilutive impact of the May equity raise; the firm kept a positive rating on the growth thesis.

news.headline: Lake Street cuts Rapid Micro Biosystems price target to $7 from $8
news.summary: Lake Street Capital Markets, a joint bookrunner on the May offering, trimmed its price target to $7 from $8 following the pricing of the new share/warrant issuance.

news.headline: Rapid Micro Biosystems Announces Pricing of Up to Approximately $32 Million Underwritten Offering
news.summary: RPID priced Class A shares with Series A/B warrants at a combined $1.955, raising about $9.8M in immediate gross proceeds (up to $32M if all warrants are later exercised for cash); Bain Capital Life Sciences, Armistice Capital and company insiders, including director Kirk Malloy, participated in the deal.

news.headline: Rapid Micro Biosystems Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
news.summary: Q1 2026 revenue rose 11% YoY to $8.0M, beating estimates, while EPS of -$0.31 missed consensus; management guided Q2 revenue to roughly $7.8M and reaffirmed full-year 2026 targets of 30-38 Growth Direct placements and $37-41M revenue.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (est. early/mid August)
catalysts.detail: Historically reports Q2 in early-to-mid August (Aug 4 in 2023, Aug 12 in 2025). Guided ~$7.8M revenue; watch new Growth Direct placement cadence toward the 30-38 unit FY26 target, consumables pull-through, and cash runway following the May equity raise and subsequent sell-side price-target cuts.

pulse: Fresh dilution-heavy stretch: a May offering (~$9.8M upfront, up to $32M if warrants are exercised) brought in Bain Capital, Armistice Capital and insider buying, but KeyBanc and Lake Street both cut price targets ($5 and $7 from $8) on the added share count, and the stock has drifted down into the ~$2 range with routine insider option-exercise sales continuing into July. Underlying business still executing on Growth Direct placements (Q1 revenue +11% YoY, FY26 guidance of 30-38 placements / $37-41M revenue reaffirmed), so the story remains a race between placement/consumables growth and continued cash burn and dilution ahead of the August Q2 report.
